speaker: BRUTUS
text: What, Lucius, ho!—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I cannot by the progress of the stars

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Give guess how near to day.—Lucius, I say!—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I would it were my fault to sleep so soundly.—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: When, Lucius, when? Awake, I say! What, Lucius!

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Called you, my lord?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Get me a taper in my study, Lucius.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: When it is lighted, come and call me here.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: I will, my lord.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: It must be by his death. And for my part

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I know no personal cause to spurn at him,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: But for the general. He would be crowned:

speaker: BRUTUS
text: How that might change his nature, there’s the question.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: It is the bright day that brings forth the adder,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And that craves wary walking. Crown him that,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And then I grant we put a sting in him

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That at his will he may do danger with.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Th’ abuse of greatness is when it disjoins

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Remorse from power. And, to speak truth of Caesar,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I have not known when his affections swayed

speaker: BRUTUS
text: More than his reason. But ’tis a common proof

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That lowliness is young ambition’s ladder,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Whereto the climber-upward turns his face;

speaker: BRUTUS
text: But, when he once attains the upmost round,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: He then unto the ladder turns his back,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Looks in the clouds, scorning the base degrees

speaker: BRUTUS
text: By which he did ascend. So Caesar may.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Then, lest he may, prevent. And since the quarrel

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Will bear no color for the thing he is,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Fashion it thus: that what he is, augmented,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Would run to these and these extremities.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And therefore think him as a serpent’s egg,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Which, hatched, would, as his kind, grow mischievous,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And kill him in the shell.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: The taper burneth in your closet, sir.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Searching the window for a flint, I found

speaker: LUCIUS
text: This paper, thus sealed up, and I am sure

speaker: LUCIUS
text: It did not lie there when I went to bed.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Get you to bed again. It is not day.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Is not tomorrow, boy, the ides of March?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: I know not, sir.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Look in the calendar, and bring me word.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: I will, sir.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The exhalations, whizzing in the air,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Give so much light that I may read by them.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Brutus, thou sleep’st. Awake, and see thyself!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Shall Rome, etc. Speak, strike, redress!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: “Brutus, thou sleep’st. Awake.”

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Such instigations have been often dropped

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Where I have took them up.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: “Shall Rome, etc.” Thus must I piece it out:

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Shall Rome stand under one man’s awe? What, Rome?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: My ancestors did from the streets of Rome

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The Tarquin drive when he was called a king.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: “Speak, strike, redress!” Am I entreated

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To speak and strike? O Rome, I make thee promise,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: If the redress will follow, thou receivest

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Thy full petition at the hand of Brutus.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Sir, March is wasted fifteen days.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: ’Tis good. Go to the gate; somebody knocks.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Since Cassius first did whet me against Caesar,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I have not slept.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Between the acting of a dreadful thing

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And the first motion, all the interim is

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Like a phantasma or a hideous dream.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The genius and the mortal instruments

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Are then in council, and the state of man,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Like to a little kingdom, suffers then

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The nature of an insurrection.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Sir, ’tis your brother Cassius at the door,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Who doth desire to see you.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Is he alone?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: No, sir. There are more with him.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Do you know them?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: No, sir. Their hats are plucked about their ears,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: And half their faces buried in their cloaks,

speaker: LUCIUS
text: That by no means I may discover them

speaker: LUCIUS
text: By any mark of favor.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Let ’em enter.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: They are the faction. O conspiracy,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Sham’st thou to show thy dang’rous brow by night,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: When evils are most free? O, then, by day

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Where wilt thou find a cavern dark enough

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To mask thy monstrous visage? Seek none, conspiracy.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Hide it in smiles and affability;

speaker: BRUTUS
text: For if thou path, thy native semblance on,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Not Erebus itself were dim enough

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To hide thee from prevention.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I think we are too bold upon your rest.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Good morrow, Brutus. Do we trouble you?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I have been up this hour, awake all night.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Know I these men that come along with you?

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Yes, every man of them; and no man here

speaker: CASSIUS
text: But honors you, and every one doth wish

speaker: CASSIUS
text: You had but that opinion of yourself

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Which every noble Roman bears of you.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: This is Trebonius.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: He is welcome hither.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: This, Decius Brutus.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: He is welcome too.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: This, Casca; this, Cinna; and this, Metellus Cimber.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: They are all welcome.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: What watchful cares do interpose themselves

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Betwixt your eyes and night?

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Shall I entreat a word?

speaker: DECIUS
text: Here lies the east; doth not the day break here?

speaker: CASCA
text: No.

speaker: CINNA
text: O pardon, sir, it doth; and yon gray lines

speaker: CINNA
text: That fret the clouds are messengers of day.

speaker: CASCA
text: You shall confess that you are both deceived.

speaker: CASCA
text: Here, as I point my sword, the sun arises,

speaker: CASCA
text: Which is a great way growing on the south,

speaker: CASCA
text: Weighing the youthful season of the year.

speaker: CASCA
text: Some two months hence, up higher toward the north

speaker: CASCA
text: He first presents his fire, and the high east

speaker: CASCA
text: Stands, as the Capitol, directly here.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Give me your hands all over, one by one.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: And let us swear our resolution.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: No, not an oath. If not the face of men,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The sufferance of our souls, the time’s abuse—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: If these be motives weak, break off betimes,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And every man hence to his idle bed.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: So let high-sighted tyranny range on

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Till each man drop by lottery. But if these—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: As I am sure they do—bear fire enough

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To kindle cowards and to steel with valor

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The melting spirits of women, then, countrymen,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: What need we any spur but our own cause

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To prick us to redress? What other bond

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Than secret Romans that have spoke the word

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And will not palter? And what other oath

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Than honesty to honesty engaged

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That this shall be or we will fall for it?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Swear priests and cowards and men cautelous,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Old feeble carrions, and such suffering souls

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That welcome wrongs; unto bad causes swear

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Such creatures as men doubt; but do not stain

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The even virtue of our enterprise,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Nor th’ insuppressive mettle of our spirits,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To think that or our cause or our performance

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Did need an oath, when every drop of blood

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That every Roman bears, and nobly bears,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Is guilty of a several bastardy

speaker: BRUTUS
text: If he do break the smallest particle

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Of any promise that hath passed from him.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: But what of Cicero? Shall we sound him?

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I think he will stand very strong with us.

speaker: CASCA
text: Let us not leave him out.

speaker: CINNA
text: No, by no means.

speaker: METELLUS
text: O, let us have him, for his silver hairs

speaker: METELLUS
text: Will purchase us a good opinion

speaker: METELLUS
text: And buy men’s voices to commend our deeds.

speaker: METELLUS
text: It shall be said his judgment ruled our hands.

speaker: METELLUS
text: Our youths and wildness shall no whit appear,

speaker: METELLUS
text: But all be buried in his gravity.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: O, name him not! Let us not break with him,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: For he will never follow anything

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That other men begin.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Then leave him out.

speaker: CASCA
text: Indeed, he is not fit.

speaker: DECIUS
text: Shall no man else be touched, but only Caesar?

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Decius, well urged. I think it is not meet

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Mark Antony, so well beloved of Caesar,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Should outlive Caesar. We shall find of him

speaker: CASSIUS
text: A shrewd contriver; and, you know, his means,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: If he improve them, may well stretch so far

speaker: CASSIUS
text: As to annoy us all; which to prevent,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Let Antony and Caesar fall together.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Our course will seem too bloody, Caius Cassius,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To cut the head off and then hack the limbs,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Like wrath in death and envy afterwards;

speaker: BRUTUS
text: For Antony is but a limb of Caesar.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Let’s be sacrificers, but not butchers, Caius.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: We all stand up against the spirit of Caesar,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And in the spirit of men there is no blood.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: O, that we then could come by Caesar’s spirit

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And not dismember Caesar! But, alas,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Caesar must bleed for it. And, gentle friends,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Let’s kill him boldly, but not wrathfully.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Let’s carve him as a dish fit for the gods,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Not hew him as a carcass fit for hounds.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And let our hearts, as subtle masters do,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Stir up their servants to an act of rage

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And after seem to chide ’em. This shall make

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Our purpose necessary and not envious;

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Which so appearing to the common eyes,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: We shall be called purgers, not murderers.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And for Mark Antony, think not of him,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: For he can do no more than Caesar’s arm

speaker: BRUTUS
text: When Caesar’s head is off.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Yet I fear him,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: For in the engrafted love he bears to Caesar—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Alas, good Cassius, do not think of him.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: If he love Caesar, all that he can do

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Is to himself: take thought and die for Caesar.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And that were much he should, for he is given

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To sports, to wildness, and much company.

speaker: TREBONIUS
text: There is no fear in him. Let him not die,

speaker: TREBONIUS
text: For he will live and laugh at this hereafter.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Peace, count the clock.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: The clock hath stricken three.

speaker: TREBONIUS
text: ’Tis time to part.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: But it is doubtful yet

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Whether Caesar will come forth today or no,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: For he is superstitious grown of late,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Quite from the main opinion he held once

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Of fantasy, of dreams, and ceremonies.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: It may be these apparent prodigies,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: The unaccustomed terror of this night,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: And the persuasion of his augurers

speaker: CASSIUS
text: May hold him from the Capitol today.

speaker: DECIUS
text: Never fear that. If he be so resolved,

speaker: DECIUS
text: I can o’ersway him, for he loves to hear

speaker: DECIUS
text: That unicorns may be betrayed with trees,

speaker: DECIUS
text: And bears with glasses, elephants with holes,

speaker: DECIUS
text: Lions with toils, and men with flatterers.

speaker: DECIUS
text: But when I tell him he hates flatterers,

speaker: DECIUS
text: He says he does, being then most flatterèd.

speaker: DECIUS
text: Let me work,

speaker: DECIUS
text: For I can give his humor the true bent,

speaker: DECIUS
text: And I will bring him to the Capitol.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Nay, we will all of us be there to fetch him.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: By the eighth hour, is that the uttermost?

speaker: CINNA
text: Be that the uttermost, and fail not then.

speaker: METELLUS
text: Caius Ligarius doth bear Caesar hard,

speaker: METELLUS
text: Who rated him for speaking well of Pompey.

speaker: METELLUS
text: I wonder none of you have thought of him.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Now, good Metellus, go along by him.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: He loves me well, and I have given him reasons.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Send him but hither, and I’ll fashion him.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: The morning comes upon ’s. We’ll leave you, Brutus.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: And, friends, disperse yourselves, but all remember

speaker: CASSIUS
text: What you have said, and show yourselves true Romans.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Good gentlemen, look fresh and merrily.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Let not our looks put on our purposes,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: But bear it, as our Roman actors do,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: With untired spirits and formal constancy.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And so good morrow to you every one.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Boy! Lucius!—Fast asleep? It is no matter.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Enjoy the honey-heavy dew of slumber.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Thou hast no figures nor no fantasies

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Which busy care draws in the brains of men.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Therefore thou sleep’st so sound.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Brutus, my lord.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Portia! What mean you? Wherefore rise you now?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: It is not for your health thus to commit

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Your weak condition to the raw cold morning.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Nor for yours neither. You’ve ungently, Brutus,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Stole from my bed. And yesternight at supper

speaker: PORTIA
text: You suddenly arose and walked about,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Musing and sighing, with your arms across,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And when I asked you what the matter was,

speaker: PORTIA
text: You stared upon me with ungentle looks.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I urged you further; then you scratched your head

speaker: PORTIA
text: And too impatiently stamped with your foot.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Yet I insisted; yet you answered not,

speaker: PORTIA
text: But with an angry wafture of your hand

speaker: PORTIA
text: Gave sign for me to leave you. So I did,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Fearing to strengthen that impatience

speaker: PORTIA
text: Which seemed too much enkindled, and withal

speaker: PORTIA
text: Hoping it was but an effect of humor,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Which sometime hath his hour with every man.

speaker: PORTIA
text: It will not let you eat nor talk nor sleep,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And could it work so much upon your shape

speaker: PORTIA
text: As it hath much prevailed on your condition,

speaker: PORTIA
text: I should not know you Brutus. Dear my lord,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Make me acquainted with your cause of grief.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I am not well in health, and that is all.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Brutus is wise and, were he not in health,

speaker: PORTIA
text: He would embrace the means to come by it.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Why so I do. Good Portia, go to bed.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Is Brutus sick? And is it physical

speaker: PORTIA
text: To walk unbracèd and suck up the humors

speaker: PORTIA
text: Of the dank morning? What, is Brutus sick,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And will he steal out of his wholesome bed

speaker: PORTIA
text: To dare the vile contagion of the night

speaker: PORTIA
text: And tempt the rheumy and unpurgèd air

speaker: PORTIA
text: To add unto his sickness? No, my Brutus,

speaker: PORTIA
text: You have some sick offense within your mind,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Which by the right and virtue of my place

speaker: PORTIA
text:
I ought to know of.
                 
                She
                 
                kneels
                .
               And upon my knees

speaker: PORTIA
text: I charm you, by my once commended beauty,

speaker: PORTIA
text: By all your vows of love, and that great vow

speaker: PORTIA
text: Which did incorporate and make us one,

speaker: PORTIA
text: That you unfold to me, your self, your half,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Why you are heavy, and what men tonight

speaker: PORTIA
text: Have had resort to you; for here have been

speaker: PORTIA
text: Some six or seven who did hide their faces

speaker: PORTIA
text: Even from darkness.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Kneel not, gentle Portia.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I should not need, if you were gentle Brutus.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Within the bond of marriage, tell me, Brutus,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Is it excepted I should know no secrets

speaker: PORTIA
text: That appertain to you? Am I your self

speaker: PORTIA
text: But, as it were, in sort or limitation,

speaker: PORTIA
text: To keep with you at meals, comfort your bed,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And talk to you sometimes? Dwell I but in the suburbs

speaker: PORTIA
text: Of your good pleasure? If it be no more,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Portia is Brutus’ harlot, not his wife.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: You are my true and honorable wife,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: As dear to me as are the ruddy drops

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That visit my sad heart.

speaker: PORTIA
text: If this were true, then should I know this secret.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I grant I am a woman, but withal

speaker: PORTIA
text: A woman that Lord Brutus took to wife.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I grant I am a woman, but withal

speaker: PORTIA
text: A woman well-reputed, Cato’s daughter.

speaker: PORTIA
text: Think you I am no stronger than my sex,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Being so fathered and so husbanded?

speaker: PORTIA
text: Tell me your counsels; I will not disclose ’em.

speaker: PORTIA
text: I have made strong proof of my constancy,

speaker: PORTIA
text: Giving myself a voluntary wound

speaker: PORTIA
text: Here, in the thigh. Can I bear that with patience,

speaker: PORTIA
text: And not my husband’s secrets?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: O you gods,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Render me worthy of this noble wife!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Hark, hark, one knocks. Portia, go in awhile,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And by and by thy bosom shall partake

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The secrets of my heart.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: All my engagements I will construe to thee,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: All the charactery of my sad brows.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Leave me with haste.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Lucius, who ’s that knocks?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Here is a sick man that would speak with you.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Caius Ligarius, that Metellus spoke of.—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Boy, stand aside.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Caius Ligarius, how?

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: Vouchsafe good morrow from a feeble tongue.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: O, what a time have you chose out, brave Caius,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To wear a kerchief! Would you were not sick!

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: I am not sick, if Brutus have in hand

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: Any exploit worthy the name of honor.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Such an exploit have I in hand, Ligarius,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Had you a healthful ear to hear of it.

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: By all the gods that Romans bow before,

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: I here discard my sickness.

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: Soul of Rome,

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: Brave son derived from honorable loins,

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: Thou like an exorcist hast conjured up

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: My mortifièd spirit. Now bid me run,

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: And I will strive with things impossible,

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: Yea, get the better of them. What’s to do?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: A piece of work that will make sick men whole.

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: But are not some whole that we must make sick?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That must we also. What it is, my Caius,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I shall unfold to thee as we are going

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To whom it must be done.

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: Set on your foot,

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: And with a heart new-fired I follow you

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: To do I know not what; but it sufficeth

speaker: LIGARIUS
text: That Brutus leads me on.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Follow me then.
